---
title: "ENVS 193DS Infographic"
date: "June 7, 2026" 
author: "Danielle Sur" 
format: pdf 
editor: source
---

# Set up

```{r}
#| message: false

# loaded in packages
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(here)
library(webr)
library(ggimage)
library(showtext)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# read in data and saved as object called `rxntime`
rxntime   <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data","new-data.csv"))

# read in my data and saved as object called 'pie'
pie <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data","pie-data.csv"))

# read in my data and saved as object called 'time'
time <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data","time-data.csv"))
```

# Part 3. Visualizing data

## Pie chart

### Cleaning data

```{r}
# created a new object from pie
pie_clean <- pie |>
  # cleaned column names
  clean_names() |> 
  # selected columns of interest
  select(exercise_type, social_interaction, count) |> 
  # changed existing abbreviations
  mutate(social_interaction = case_when(
    social_interaction == "y" ~ "Yes",
    social_interaction == "n" ~ "No"
  )) |> 
  # removed missing values
  drop_na()
```

### Visualization

```{r}
# created a pie donut chart using the webr package and pie_clean df
piechart <- PieDonut(data = pie_clean,
         # pie slices by exercise type, outlined by social interaction
         aes(exercise_type, social_interaction, count = count),
         # outer circle of the pie adds up to 100%
         r0 = 0.5, # inner ring thickness
         r1 = 1, # outer ring thickness
         ratioByGroup = FALSE,
         title = "Social Interaction Percentage by Exercise Type")
```

## Box / jitter plot

### Cleaning data

```{r}
# created a new object from rxntime
rxntime_box <- rxntime |>
  # cleaned column names
  clean_names() |> 
  # selected columns of interest
  select(exercise_type, reaction_time_avg_ms) |> 
  # changed existing abbreviations
  mutate(case_when(
    exercise_type == "volleyball" ~ "Volleyball",
    exercise_type == "biking" ~ "Biking",
    exercise_type == "walking" ~ "Walking",
    TRUE ~ as.character(exercise_type))) |> 
    mutate(image = img_map[exercise_type]) |> 
 
  # removed missing values
  drop_na()
```

### Visualization

```{r}
rxntime_ <- rxntime_clean |> 
  mutate(exercise_type = as.character(exercise_type),
         image = img_map[exercise_type])
```

```{r}
# base layer: ggplot
boxplot <- ggplot(data = rxntime_box,
       # x-axis: exercise type
       # y-axis: mean reaction time
       # color by exercise type
       mapping = aes(x = exercise_type,
                     y = reaction_time_avg_ms,
                     color = exercise_type)) +
  # first layer: boxplot
  geom_boxplot() +
  # second layer: icons and jitter
  geom_image(
    aes(image = image),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.15,
                               height = 0),
    size = 0.045,
    asp = 1) +
  # relabeled x- and y-axes
  labs(x = "Exercise Type",
       y = "Mean Reaction Time (ms)") +
  # removed legend
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  # custom colors
  scale_color_manual(values = c("biking" = "slateblue",
                                "volleyball" = "dodgerblue",
                                "walking" = "lightskyblue")) +
  # removed grid lines
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(color = "black"))
```

## Scatter plot

### Cleaning data

```{r}
# created a new object from rxntime
rxntime_clean <- rxntime |> 
  # cleaned column names
  clean_names() |> 
  # selected columns of interest
  select(exercise_type, exercise_duration_min, reaction_time_avg_ms) |> 
  # removed missing values
  drop_na()
```

### Visualization

```{r}
# base layer: ggplot
scatterplot <- ggplot(data = rxntime_clean,
       # x-axis: exercise duration in minutes
       # y-axis: average reaction time in milliseconds
       # color
       mapping = aes(x = "Exercise Type",
                     y = "Average Reaction Time (ms)")) +
  # first layer: data points
  geom_point(data = rxntime_clean,
             aes(x = exercise_duration_min,
                 y = reaction_time_avg_ms,
                 size = 2,
                 color = "pink")) +
  # changed theme
  theme_minimal() +
  # removed legend
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.background = element_blank(), # removing panel background
        panel.grid = element_blank(), # removing panel grid
        axis.ticks = element_blank(), # removing axis ticks
    ) +
  # relabeled x- and y- axes, adding title
  labs(title = "Exercise Duration and Reaction Time",
       subtitle = "May 29, 2026",
       x = "Exercise Duration (min)",
       y = "Average Reaction Time (ms)") +
  # custom colors
  scale_color_manual(values = "dodgerblue")
```

```{r}
img_map <- c(
  biking = "images/biking.png",
  volleyball = "images/vb.png",
  walking = "images/walking.png"
)

rxntime_clean <- rxntime_clean |> 
  clean_names() |> 
  mutate(exercise_type = as.character(exercise_type),
         image = img_map[exercise_type])
```

```{r}
# base layer: ggplot
ggplot(rxntime_clean,
       # x-axis: exercise duration in minutes
       # y-axis: average reaction time in milliseconds
       aes(x = exercise_duration_min,
           y = reaction_time_avg_ms)) +
  # first layer: data points represented by custom icons
  geom_image(
    aes(image = image), # icon placement
    size = 0.045, # icon size
    asp = 1) + # square aspect ratio
  # custom theme
  theme_minimal()
```

## Image export

```{r}
# transparent PNG export
ggsave("pie.png",
       plot = piechart,
       bg = "transparent",
       width = 4,
       height = 3,
       dpi = 300)

ggsave("boxjitter.png",
       plot = boxplot,
       device = ragg::agg_png,
       bg = "transparent",
       width = 4,
       height = 3,
       dpi = 300)

ggsave("scatter.png",
       plot = scatterplot,
       device = ragg::agg_png,
       bg = "transparent",
       width = 4,
       height = 3,
       dpi = 300)
```

# Part 4. Write about your process

A classmate on Friday used birds to represent raw data points.
